action.hypothesis: JumpReLU tuning appears fundamentally stalled, so the next credible move is to test the gated SAE family at K=128, where a separate sigmoid routing branch may learn cleaner support patterns than JumpReLU and reduce FVU under the same strong optimization settings.
action.summary: Switch from exhausted JumpReLU sweeps to a proxy gated-family prototype at K=128 using the current best optimizer and training settings as the control.
action.self_review: This satisfies the severe-stagnation requirement to leave minor JumpReLU tweaks and try a fundamentally different architecture. The main risk is another gated-family failure, but that is more informative now than continuing a search direction that has stalled for six rounds.
notes_to_memory: Six consecutive non-improving rounds indicate the local JumpReLU search direction is exhausted for now.
This round promotes the incubating gated family back into active testing as the required fundamentally different architecture probe.
Keeping the best-known training regime fixed isolates whether routing design, rather than another optimizer tweak, is the missing ingredient for lower FVU.
next_hypotheses: If gated improves proxy FVU at acceptable throughput, stabilize the family by tuning gate-specific parameters such as temperature or init_logit.
If gated shows quality promise but runtime regresses, follow with a code/performance round instead of discarding the architecture outright.
If gated is clearly worse or crashes again, retire it and move to a different architecture family rather than returning to more JumpReLU-local tuning.
